docs.title: Audit: Studies & Research (April 2026)
docs.text: A critical self-review of the AFT research corpus — strengths, gaps, and priority order for closing the remaining evidence. Intended both as an internal compass and as reviewer-anticipation prep for the arXiv submission.

docs.title: 1. Snapshot
docs.text:
AFT is shipped as a five-layer emotional memory library with a theory stack (philosophy → neuroscience → computational models → design principles), 9 families of empirical studies, a pre-registration + 11 addenda (B/D/E/F/G/H/I/J/K/Hd2-PowerTopUp/M), and a canonical claim matrix. The public claim ceiling, per 10_scientific_quality_bar.md, is currently "strong theory-driven prototype with early controlled evidence + one honest external negative." Of the seven tracked claims in claim_validation_matrix.json, three have controlled/early evidence, one carries strong intra-theory evidence, one is implemented only, and one is not_established; none carry ecological validation yet.

Quality gate status (2026-05-13, updated from 2026-05-06): - Gate 1 (external benchmark): CLOSED — LoCoMo H1/H2 FAIL, honest negative result documented. Add. J Pareto sweep (Hj1 FAIL 2026-05-06): no AFT config ≥ naive_rag on any category; per-task base_weights tuning line closed. - Gate 2 (human eval): OPEN — kit shipped (benchmarks/human_eval/), zero rater ratings collected. - Gate 3 (architecture attribution): CLOSED — Hd1 PASS (Addendum D), aft_noAppraisal Δ=+0.23 vs naive_cosine. - Add. G — Hg1 LLM dual-path (CLOSED 2026-05-07, FAIL): Pre-registered study on LLM dual-path with real appraisal engine. Hg1 FAIL — LLM dual-path degrades realistic-recall top1 (Δ=−0.03 to −0.05 across embedders). Synchronous-keyword destructive override the dominant pattern. See benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_g_closure.md. - Add. K — Hk1 DailyDialog ecological replication (CLOSED 2026-05-07, FAIL Branch B): N=120 synthetic personas, 396 affect-conditioned queries, multilingual-e5-small. AFT top1=0.212 vs naive_cosine=0.220 (Δ=−0.008, p_holm=1.000, d=−0.015). AFT advantage is regime-specific (curated affective benchmarks); does not extend to naturalistic short-turn dialogue. Exploratory: affective_trajectory Δ=+0.103 (N=39, underpowered). See benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_k_dailydialog_closure.md. - Hd2-PowerTopUp (CLOSED 2026-05-07, FAIL-FAIL Branch C): Power top-up to N=120 for Italian and Spanish on multilingual-e5-small. IT Δ=+0.058 p=0.276; ES Δ=0.000 p=1.000 — both FAIL at declared power. Cross-language evidence scoped to Spanish-SBERT N=80 (exploratory positive only). See benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_hd2_powertopup_closure.md. - Add. M — Hm1 French slice (CLOSED 2026-05-16, PASS Branch A): Pre-registered FR realistic_recall_v2 (me5, N=120, 30 hand-authored native scenarios, 2-session). AFT top1=0.31 vs naive_cosine=0.12 (Δ=+0.18 [0.11, 0.26], p<0.0001, g=0.424). Cross-language evidence is now two-positive (Spanish-SBERT exploratory + French-me5 confirmatory). See benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_m_fr_closure.md. - Add. I — Hi3 confirmatory (CLOSED 2026-05-06, PASS): N=500 confirms cross-embedder amplification on semantic_confound (Δ=+0.090, d=0.257, Holm p=0.0234). Hi3_recency PASS; Hi3_arc FAIL. Mechanism (Hi2: link-type/strength differentiation) remains exploratory. See benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_i_closure.md.

docs.title: Research documents → role in the theory-to-evidence pyramid
docs.text: # File Role 01 01_foundations.md Why emotion is a memory dimension (Aristotle → enactivism) 02 02_neuroscience.md Dual-path encoding, arousal consolidation, appraisal, reconsolidation 03 03_computational_models.md 20+ prior models; identifies the gap AFT targets 04 04_state_of_art.md LLM memory systems; positions AFT against Mem0/MemGPT/Letta 05 05_design_principles.md 5 layers + 9 principles; pseudocode for core operations 06 06_bibliography.md ~120 references across disciplines 07 07_related_work.md 29-system comparative table 08 08_limitations.md Honest account of what is not yet covered 09 09_current_evidence.md Live evidence matrix; study ladder 10 10_scientific_quality_bar.md Pre-committed gates before upgrading the claim

docs.title: Benchmark families → evidence level
docs.text: Family Path Evidence level Fidelity benchmarks/fidelity/ 1 — theory fidelity (126 cases, 20 phenomena) Comparative benchmarks/comparative/ 2 — controlled retrieval (SBERT + hash) Appraisal quality benchmarks/appraisal_quality/ 3 — appraisal, LLM-dependent Appraisal confound benchmarks/appraisal_confound/ 2–4 — architecture attribution (Gate 3) Realistic replay benchmarks/realistic/ 4 — multi-session (N=100, v1.4) Ablation benchmarks/ablation/ 4 — layer isolation LoCoMo benchmarks/locomo/ 4–5 — external conversational QA (Gate 1) Human eval benchmarks/human_eval/ 5 — human ecological (Gate 2) Performance benchmarks/perf/ — (non-scientific; throughput/latency)

docs.title: 3. Strengths
docs.text:
• Theory-fidelity density. 126 cases across 20 phenomena in benchmarks/fidelity/ directly validate Russell mood-congruence, Yerkes- Dodson arousal modulation, ACT-R power-law decay, spacing effect, spreading activation, and state-dependent recall.
  • Pre-registered evidence programme. preregistration.md (S1 LoCoMo, S2 realistic v2) + preregistration_addendum_v2.md (Addenda A/B/C) commit hypotheses, effect-size targets, and Holm correction before results.
  • Claim-ceiling discipline. claim_validation_matrix.json freezes the "allowed public wording" per claim and ties each to evidence, limitations, and the next study — forcing paper and README to match evidence, not ambition.
  • Reproducibility primitives. benchmarks/common/statistics.py (bootstrap, McNemar, Holm), per-run results.protocol.json, deterministic seeds, dual embedders (hash + sbert-bge). The .dryrun.* convention in benchmarks/locomo/ gitignores partial runs so they cannot be cited as evidence.
  • Architecture attribution pre-scaffolded. benchmarks/appraisal_confound/ anticipates the single most obvious reviewer objection ("AFT wins because the LLM appraisal leaks affect into the query") and wires a no-LLM keyword- appraisal condition to test it. Runner is implemented and awaiting execution.
  • One per-challenge result survives Holm. On the realistic replay semantic_confound subset (N=30), AFT top1 = 0.73 vs naive_cosine = 0.47, Δ = +0.27 [0.10, 0.43], p_adj = 0.006 — the first per-slice AFT advantage that survives multiple-testing correction.

docs.title: 4. Gaps and risks
docs.text:
Each gap is named, evidence-backed, and ranked by how much it constrains the public claim ceiling.

G1 — No human-ecological evidence (blocks Gate 2). benchmarks/human_eval/ has packets, protocol, and a rater-instruction file, but no ratings.jsonl. Claim models_human_emotional_memory stays at not_established. Nothing in the corpus shows that humans perceive AFT output as more coherent or useful than a cosine baseline.

G2 — LoCoMo run completed; Gate 1 did NOT pass (negative result, now committed). benchmarks/locomo/results.json and results.md committed 2026-04-27. Full 10-conversation run (1986 QA pairs), both aft and naive_rag:

System N F1 BLEU-1 Judge Acc aft 1540 0.168 0.169 0.279 naive_rag 1540 0.271 0.263 0.441

Hypothesis tests (pre-reg S1, Holm-Bonferroni n_bootstrap=2000): - H1 (F1): Δ = −0.101, p_one = 1.0 → FAIL - H2 (judge_acc): Δ = −0.159, p_one = 1.0 → FAIL - Gate 1: FAIL

Interpretation: AFT retrieval is optimised for affective relevance, not factual QA. On LoCoMo — where the gold answer is an explicit factual statement from a specific turn — mood-congruent and appraisal-weighted retrieval actively mis-prioritises emotionally salient but factually irrelevant turns. This is an honest negative result. The "external benchmark advantage" wording remains prohibited; 10_scientific_quality_bar.md status updated accordingly. Claim locomo_external_qa_negative added to claim_validation_matrix.json.

Add. J Pareto sweep — CLOSED 2026-05-06. Hj1 FAIL. Pre-registered base_weights tuning study (10 configs × 200-QA stratified subsample, seed=42). No AFT config achieves F1 ≥ naive_rag on any category. Best: W2 (sem=0.50, mood=0.30, no resonance) aggregate F1=0.1765 vs naive_rag=0.2092. Per-task base_weights tuning line closed. See benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_j_closure.md.

G3 — CLOSED 2026-04-27. Appraisal confound resolved. Confirmatory run (seed=1, Addendum D Hd1) committed to benchmarks/appraisal_confound/results.confirmatory.{json,md}.

Results (SBERT, N=100, seed=1):

System top1 aft_noAppraisal 0.780 naive_cosine 0.550 aft_keyword 0.160

Hd1 PASS: aft_noAppraisal (0.78) > naive_cosine (0.55), Δ=+0.23 [+0.12, +0.34], d=0.515 — effect replicates with new seed. AFT architecture (preset affect, no appraisal) reliably outperforms naive cosine.

Gate 3 interpretation: the realistic-replay advantage is attributable to the AFT retrieval architecture, not to LLM-injected appraisal signals. aft_keyword (0.16) confirms that keyword appraisal degrades preset affect (same destructive-override finding as G3 discovery and Addendum F).

Initial study (2026-04-26, seed=42, SBERT, N=100): benchmarks/appraisal_confound/results.json — Ha2 FAIL (aft_keyword vs naive_cosine, Δ=−0.39), Hb2 FAIL (|aft_keyword − aft_noAppraisal|=0.62). Pre-registered hypotheses both failed because keyword appraisal destructively overrides hand-curated preset affect. The descriptive architecture-attribution comparison (aft_noAppraisal vs naive_cosine, Δ≈+0.23 [+0.12, +0.33]) was the signal that motivated Hd1 as the confirmatory re-pre-registration.

G4 — ✅ CLOSED (2026-04-27): Statistical power confirmed at N=200. Realistic replay v2 (50 scenarios, 200 queries, 5 challenge types × 40). SBERT bge-small: AFT top1=0.53 vs naive_cosine=0.33, Δ=+0.205 [0.150,0.265], p_bootstrap<0.001, d=0.49. Per challenge type (SBERT):

Challenge N AFT top1 naive top1 Δ [95% CI] semantic_confound 40 0.72 0.47 +0.25 affective_arc 40 0.42 0.15 +0.27 momentum_alignment 40 0.60 0.33 +0.27 same_topic_distractor 40 0.75 0.62 +0.13 recency_confound 40 0.15 0.05 +0.10

Aggregate bootstrap CI excludes 0 on all metrics. G4 is resolved.

G5 — ✅ CLOSED (2026-04-27): Cross-embedder advantage confirmed. v2 cross-embedder results: SBERT Δ=+0.205 (d=0.49); e5-small-v2 Δ=+0.155 (d=0.31). Both embedder classes yield consistent positive advantage with bootstrap CI excluding 0. The earlier hash-embedder inversion at v1.4 was a hash-embedder artefact — dense-embedding-conditional scope remains honest, but the claim now holds on two meaningfully different dense embedder classes. Results: benchmarks/realistic/results.v2.{sbert,e5}.{json,md}.

G6 — Language coverage. ✓ CLOSED (2026-05-02) Italian multilingual slice executed on two embedder configurations: SBERT all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (EN-centric) and intfloat/multilingual-e5-small. AFT hit@k advantage is significant on both (p=0.0005 SBERT, p=0.001 me5); delta is consistent at ≈+0.15–0.16. Cross-embedder robustness documented in 12_multilingual_followup.md and benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_h.md. Language claims now extend to "Italian and English conversational text". See also Addendum M FR (Hm1 PASS, 2026-05-16): French me5 N=120 confirms cross-language effect.

G7 — Dimensional coverage. ✓ CLOSED 2026-05-05 CoreAffect is now 3D PAD (valence × arousal × dominance), shipped in v0.8.2 (commit 8b9ddbe). AppraisalVector.coping_potential populates CoreAffect.dominance; MoodField uses dominance directly (no heuristic). Default dominance = 0.5 when no appraisal engine is active. No study yet tests whether the dominance dimension adds discriminative power over the 2D baseline — the omission of a dominance-specific study is now a deliberate choice, disclosed in §Affect model scope of the paper.

G8 — Paper narrative risk. ✓ CLOSED 2026-05-05 The ceiling-effect framing and the controlled-vs-realistic distinction are now explicitly stated in paper/main.tex §Comparative Benchmark → Results (around "The tie at $N=20$ reflects a dense-embedder ceiling effect…"). Both the controlled tie (0.80 vs 0.80, Δ=0.00, N=20) and the realistic win (0.53 vs 0.33, Δ=+0.21, N=200) are explained together; reviewers are not left to reconstruct the argument.

G9 — Under-benchmarked implemented mechanisms. ✓ CLOSED 2026-04-26 Dual-path encoding and APE-gated reconsolidation ablations executed (pre-reg v3, Addendum E; benchmarks/ablation/results.sbert.{json,md,protocol.json}).

Results (SBERT, N=100, seed=0):

Variant top1 Δ vs full Holm p_adj He verdict full 0.70 — — baseline no_reconsolidation 0.70 0.00 1.000 He2 FAIL dual_path 0.35 −0.35 0.000 He1 FAIL

He2 (no_reconsolidation): Δ=0.00, p_adj=1.000 — reconsolidation is implemented per theory (Nader & Schiller 2000) but does not measurably affect retrieval on this benchmark. The benchmark may not exercise the conditions that trigger reconsolidation windows (high APE at retrieval).

He1 (dual_path): Δ=−0.35, p_adj≈0 — as expected (caveat documented in pre-reg v3): the KeywordAppraisalEngine degrades preset affect at the slow-path elaborate step (same destructive-override finding from G3/Addendum A). He1 measures "no appraisal" vs "deferred bad appraisal" — not the more informative "does deferral mitigate synchronous keyword destruction?", which requires a synchronous-keyword baseline. Addendum F CLOSED (2026-04-27) — see below.

Addendum F — Hf1 CLOSED 2026-04-27 (benchmarks/preregistration_addendum_f.md)

aft_keyword_synchronous variant added: synchronous KeywordAppraisalEngine at encode time, no elaborate(). Tests Hf1: dual_path.top1 > aft_keyword_synchronous.top1.

Results (SBERT, N=100, seed=0):

Variant top1 Δ vs full Hf1 dual_path 0.350 −0.35 — aft_keyword_synchronous 0.070 −0.63 — Δ_Hf1 (dual_path − sync) — +0.280 PASS

Hf1 PASS: Deferring keyword appraisal to the slow path reduces destructive override (0.35 vs 0.07). The LeDoux dual-path architecture provides a measurable benefit relative to synchronous keyword appraisal, even though both are inferior to no appraisal (preset affect only). Note: aft_keyword_synchronous actual=0.07 is slightly below the pre-registered expected range (0.10–0.20); destructive override was more severe on this call path than anticipated.

docs.title: 5. Theory–evidence coherence check
docs.text:
Layer Tested by Coverage Core affect (Russell) fidelity, comparative, realistic strong Affective momentum fidelity/test_momentum.py, ablation (no_momentum) direct Mood field (Heidegger) fidelity/test_mood_congruent.py, fidelity/test_mood_adaptive.py, ablation (no_mood) direct Appraisal (Scherer) appraisal_quality/, ablation (no_appraisal), appraisal_confound scaffolded, thin (LLM-dependent) Resonance (spreading + Hebbian) fidelity/test_spreading_activation.py, ablation (no_resonance) direct Dual-path encoding (LeDoux) ablation dual_path (He1 FAIL; expected) + Addendum F Hf1 PASS (dual_path > synchronous) closed APE-gated reconsolidation ablation no_reconsolidation (He2 FAIL; benchmark doesn't exercise reconsolidation triggers) closed (null result)

All seven layers benchmarked. Dual-path shows null vs full (expected: keyword appraisal destructive) but Hf1 PASS confirms deferred appraisal outperforms synchronous — the LeDoux dual-path architecture provides measurable benefit at the appraisal-timing level. Reconsolidation shows null result on this dataset (triggers not exercised).

docs.title: 6. Recommended priority order
docs.text:
Ordered by evidence-per-cost and by which gap most constrains the claim ceiling. Each item is framed as "do X before claiming Y."

  1. Execute appraisal_confound (Gate 3). ✓ DONE 2026-04-27. Hd1 PASS: aft_noAppraisal (0.78) > naive_cosine (0.55), Δ=+0.23 with new seed. "Architecture drives the advantage" is now claimable.
  2. ~~Execute LoCoMo S1 (Gate 1).~~ Completed 2026-04-27. Gate 1 NOT passed. AFT F1=0.168 vs naive_rag=0.271. External-benchmark advantage wording remains prohibited. See benchmarks/locomo/results.json.
  3. Ship the human-eval pilot (Gate 2). Even a minimal N=5 raters × 10 scenarios lifts claim models_human_emotional_memory out of not_established. Addendum C already specifies Krippendorff α ≥ 0.67 as the publishability criterion.
  4. ~~Expand realistic replay to v2, N≥200, with cross-embedder slice.~~ Completed 2026-04-27. G4 + G5 both closed. SBERT Δ=+0.205 d=0.49, e5-small-v2 Δ=+0.155 d=0.31. Per-challenge advantage confirmed at N=40/type.
  5. Add a dedicated ablation for dual-path encoding and APE-reconsolidation. ✓ Done 2026-04-26 (G9 CLOSED). He1/He2 both FAIL on realistic_recall_v1.4 — null results are documented with honest interpretation. Paper's layer-by-layer contribution table is now complete.
  6. ~~Multilingual slice (Addendum B).~~ ✓ Done 2026-05-02 (Addendum H). Italian SBERT + me5-small both executed. hit@k SIG on both embedders (p=0.0005 / p=0.001). Cross-embedder robustness confirmed. Language claims updated to "Italian and English conversational text". Addendum M (2026-05-16) extends to French (Hm1 PASS, me5 N=120, Δ=+0.18 p<0.0001 g=0.424).

docs.title: 7. Reviewer anticipation (paper-specific)
docs.text:
Five questions a skeptical arXiv reader will ask, with the strongest currently-defensible answer and the honest remaining gap.

Q1 — "Does the advantage come from your architecture or from your LLM appraisal leaking affect into the query?" → Current answer: Closed (Gate 3, G3, 2026-04-27). aft_noAppraisal (pure preset affect, no appraisal engine) achieves top1=0.78 vs naive_cosine 0.55, Δ=+0.23 [+0.12, +0.34], d=0.52, seed=1 confirmatory. The advantage holds without any LLM involvement — it is architectural.

Q2 — "Does AFT beat cosine on real human conversation, not just on a benchmark you built?" → Current answer: No — LoCoMo S1 ran on 1986 QA pairs and AFT underperformed naive_rag (F1 0.168 vs 0.271, Gate 1 FAIL). This is an honest negative result: AFT's affective weighting does not help on factual open-domain conversational QA. The advantage remains restricted to controlled and realistic affect-oriented replay benchmarks. Gap (updated): Gate 1 is closed with a negative result. The claim ceiling is unchanged; LoCoMo advantage wording is still prohibited.

Q3 — "Do humans actually prefer AFT's output?" → Current answer: unknown — human_eval packet is ready, no ratings collected. Gap: Gate 2 (G1).

Q4 — "Why does AFT tie cosine on your controlled probe but beat it on your realistic probe?" → Current answer: the controlled probe hits a dense-embedder ceiling at N=20 (both systems at 0.80); the N=200 realistic probe discriminates (Δ=+0.21, d=0.49, SBERT; Δ=+0.16, d=0.31, e5-small-v2). Gap: ~~stated once in 09_current_evidence.md but not framed explicitly in paper/main.tex (G8)~~ — G8 CLOSED 2026-05-05: explicitly framed in paper/main.tex §Comparative Benchmark → Results.

Q5 — "Does this work outside English, on a sparse embedder, with the dominance dimension?" → Current answer (updated 2026-05-05): - Multilingual: Italian (G6, Hd2_IT PASS, Δ=+0.163, p=0.012) and Spanish (Hd2_ES SBERT PASS, Δ=+0.138, p=0.045) confirmed; me5-small ES borderline (Δ=+0.113, p=0.110). French (Hm1, Addendum M, 2026-05-16) confirms me5 at N=120 (Δ=+0.18, p<0.0001, g=0.424). Scope extended to "English + Italian + Spanish + French + dense embedding". - Sparse embedder (G5): still scoped out; hash-embedder gap Δ≈+0.06 ns. Documented in §Language and embedding scope of the paper. - Dominance (G7): shipped in v0.8.2 — CoreAffect is now 3D PAD. No study yet isolates dominance's discriminative contribution. Remaining gap: G5 (sparse embedder), dominance ablation study (no pre-reg yet). G6 and G7 are closed.

docs.title: Claim Validation Matrix
docs.text:
The claim validation matrix is the authoritative record of all empirical claims made by the Affective Field Theory (AFT) library, their current evidence status, and the references to benchmark artefacts that support or qualify each claim.

Each entry carries:

  • claim_text — the exact public-facing claim
  • requires_oracle_affect — true if the claim's primary positive-result benchmarks inject preset valence/arousal at encode time (oracle affect); false if the benchmark ran without oracle injection (naturalistic or appraisal-driven). Do not conflate oracle-affect results with end-to-end naturalistic results — they measure different things.
  • evidence_refs / benchmark_refs / protocol_refs / limitations_refs — file paths within the repository that serve as evidence (all paths validated in CI via tools/audit_claim_refs.py)
  • current_evidence — evidence classification (e.g. strong_intra_theory, strong_cross_task, not_yet_shown)
  • not_yet_shown — explicit scope limitations attached to each claim
  • allowed_public_wording — the precise wording permitted in communications
  • next_study — reference to any planned follow-up pre-registration

Download raw JSON{ .md-button }

docs.title: Validation tool
docs.text:
uv run python tools/audit_claim_refs.py

Exits 0 if all referenced paths exist on disk; exits 1 with a summary of missing paths. Integrated into make meta-check and therefore into make check.

docs.title: Figure Inventory
docs.text:
Source of truth: figure_inventory.json. This document is a human-readable view — edit the JSON, not this table.

Three distinct categories of figures are produced by three separate generators. Each generator writes to its own directory; they do not cross-populate each other's directories by default.

docs.title: Category roles
docs.text:
Category Generator Output directory Format Purpose didactic scripts/generate_docs_images.py docs/images/ PNG API docs, README, docs site evidence scripts/generate_research_figures.py docs/images/research/ PNG + PDF Research docs, benchmark evidence paper scripts/generate_paper_figures.py paper/figures/ PDF paper/main.tex only

To place evidence figures inside paper/figures/, invoke the generator explicitly with --pdf-dir paper/figures. This is not done automatically.

docs.title: Regeneration commands
docs.text:
make docs-images       # didactic figures only
make research-figures  # evidence figures only
make paper-figures     # paper figures only
make figures           # all three

All didactic figures use synthetic/hardcoded inputs — no benchmark JSON.
